notes: Staged LLM extraction directly from each source span. Consumes only the span text and declared source metadata; never the manual object set or expected_* labels. Review priority is assigned application-side by type.

predictions.predicted_statement: New clients are required to pay the standard deposit before scheduling can be finalized, unless an approved exception applies.
After-hours scheduling must be approved by the operations lead, unless it is an emergency priority incident.
When active water damage, loss of heat, or another safety issue is present, dispatch may bypass the normal after‑hours approval step.
VIP maintenance clients may bypass the usual deposit when the dispatcher confirms the account and records the justification.
Before confirming a job, check if a documented exception applies and, if so, record the waiver reason in the job record.

predictions.predicted_title: Emergency dispatch classification and availability check
predictions.predicted_statement: Emergency dispatch must classify the incident and verify technician availability.
Long-term maintenance clients are typically identified by an active maintenance plan and a service visit within the past year.
The VIP client list is the internal roster of long‑term maintenance accounts that receive deposit flexibility and expedited handling.
Emergency priority applies only to active damage, safety hazards, or service outages that affect core habitability.

predictions.predicted_title: Notify field lead if technician unavailable
predictions.predicted_statement: If no qualified technician is available for a same‑day emergency, the dispatcher must notify the field lead.
Deposit exceptions must be recorded in the job record prior to confirming scheduling.
